title: PingOne Advanced Identity Cloud
description:
This is an overview of your PingOne Advanced Identity Cloud tenant security events. From here you can discover insights on event types, trends over time, region info, and more.

For further information, see:

- [Monitoring Activity with PingOne Advanced Identity Cloud](https://www.datadoghq.com/blog/monitor-activity-with-okta/)

- [PingOne Advanced Identity Cloud Integration Documentation](https://docs.datadoghq.com/integrations/okta/)

- [PingOne Advanced Identity Cloud Event Type API Documentation](https://developer.okta.com/docs/reference/api/event-types/) (cloned)

definition.content:
Use this dashboard to monitor your PingOne Advanced Identity Cloud environments, giving you greater visibility into security-related events from all of your journeys, applications and users. With PingOne Advanced Identity Cloud and Datadog, you can proactively detect threats to your applications, track user and administrator activity, debug authentication and authorization issues, and create an audit trail for regulatory compliance.

The PingOne Advanced Identity Cloud integration pulls the following log sources for each configured environment: _am-authentication_, _am-config_, _am_activity_,  _idm-activity_, and _idm-config_. The log source name is available in the log's `@evt.category` attribute.

Filters are available to search on specific realms, journeys, client IP addresses, principal names, and event outcomes.

For further reading, see:

-   [Advanced Identity Cloud Log Sources](https://docs.pingidentity.com/pingoneaic/tenants/audit-debug-log-sources.html#log-source-descriptions)

Datadog Cloud SIEM analyzes and correlates PingOne Advanced Identity Cloud logs to detect threats to your environment in real time. If you don't see signals please make sure you're signed up for [Datadog Cloud SIEM](https://app.datadoghq.com/security).
The PingOne Advanced Identity Cloud log source _idm-activity_ reports changes made to users and objects such as applications.

User Login activity is defined as any interaction with the _Login_ journey or any other authentication-related journey defined in the PingOne Advanced Identity Cloud environment. Note, you should clone this dashboard and update the query for the Login Activity timeseries to add any journey references other than _Login_. See the query segment `@payload.entries.info.treeName:(Login OR "Login Public")` as an example of adding a journey named _Login Public_ to the query.

Applications include PingOne Advanced Identity Cloud internal components, such as _org-filter-client_, and customer defined applications/OAuth2 clients.

Application activity is evident in the AM-LOGIN-MODULE-COMPLETED and AM-LOGIN-COMPLETED events.

definition.title: Application Activity by Principal and Outcome

definition.content: Administrator activity is gathered from the _am-config_ log source as well as authentication journey activity on the `/` realm.
